rag_rules: Preserve the AADL architecture by inserting AGREE annex blocks only in valid component type or implementation scopes
Place component type annexes in the component type scope and implementation annexes in the implementation scope after subcomponents and connections
Use only visible identifiers from the current scope when writing AGREE contracts
Do not use subcomponent paths in component type annexes
generic_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks in the appropriate scope to maintain architectural integrity
Preserve the existing AADL structure when adding AGREE contracts
Use only visible identifiers from the current scope when writing AGREE contracts
Place implementation-level bindings in the implementation scope after subcomponents and connections
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Component type annexes are for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equation declarations
Implementation annexes are for implementation-level bindings such as assignments
anti_patterns: Do not use subcomponent paths in component type annexes
Do not use AADL property names directly in AGREE expressions without explicit typed ports or constants
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless the model exposes explicit typed ports or constants for them
irrelevant_topics: AGREE, annex, assume, guarantee, assign, validator, or repair
